localizedDescriptions.en: Document to communicate information to others, commonly in response to a request from another party.
Identifiable details of a person compliant with European ISA standard.
A unique personal identifier.
Personal name compliant with European ISA standard.
A family name is usually shared by members of a family. This attribute also carries prefixes or suffixes which are part of the Family Name, e.g. “de Boer”, “van de Putte”, “von und zu Orlow”.

annotations.comment: Multiple family names, such as are commonly found in Hispanic countries, are recorded in the single Family Name field so that, for example, Miguel de Cervantes Saavedra's Family Name would be recorded as "de Cervantes Saavedra.

localizedDescriptions.en: A given name, or multiple given names, are the denominator(s) that identify an individual within a family. These are given to a person by his or her parents at birth or may be legally recognised as 'given names' through a formal process.

annotations.comment: All given names are ordered in one field so that, for example, the Given Name for Johan Sebastian Bach is 'Johan Sebastian.'

localizedDescriptions.en: The person's date of birth.
Anonymised details of a person.
Ther current administrative gender of the person.
The sex / gender is male.
The sex / gnder is female.
The sex/ gender is indeterminate.
The age of the person. This may be calculated.
Details of a healthcare professional.
Personal name compliant with European ISA standard.

The full name (fullName) property contains the complete name of a person as one string. In addition to the content of given name, family name and, in some systems, patronymic name, this can carry additional parts of a person’s name such as titles, middle names or suffixes like “the third” or names which are neither a given nor a family name.
The healthcare worker's profesional identifier.
Details of a healthcare provider organisation.
The name of the organisation.
The unique identifier of the organisation.
Address details compliant with European ISA Standard.
The key postal division of the address, usually the city.
The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities.
The uppermost administrative unit for the address, almost always a country.
The name of a specific department within the organisation.
The identifier of a specific department.
Contact details for the organisation.
Contact details for the heathcare worker.
A generic section header which should be renamed in a template to suit a specific clinical context.
A clinical activity that has been carried out for therapeutic or diagnostic purposes.
The name of the procedure.
Details of a healthcare provider organisation.
The name of the organisation.
The unique identifier of the organisation.
Address details compliant with European ISA Standard.
The key postal division of the address, usually the city.
The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities.
The uppermost administrative unit for the address, almost always a country.
The name of a specific department within the organisation.
The identifier of a specific department.
Contact details for the organisation.
Request for procedure is initiated.
Request for procedure sent.
Request for procedure is postponed.
Procedure request has been cancelled.
Procedure has been scheduled.
Procedure is being carried out.
Procedure has been suspended.
Procedure has been aborted.
Procedure has been completed.
Procedure report has been written.
Procedure report has been attested.
Procedure report has been distributed.
Positive statement/s about procedures that need to be recorded as clinically excluded from the health record at a specific point in time.
A statement about exclusion of procedures performed in the health record.

annotations.comment: For example: "No known operations or significant procedures" or "No previous" (appendicectomy).

localizedDescriptions.en: A generic section header which should be renamed in a template to suit a specific clinical context.
A clinical activity that has been carried out for therapeutic or diagnostic purposes.
The name of the procedure.
The reason or indication for the procedure.

children.name: Hip joint implant

localizedDescriptions.en: An instrument, apparatus, implant, material or similar, used in the provision of healthcare. In this context, a medical device includes a broad range of devices which act through a variety of physical, mechanical, thermal or similar means but specifically excludes devices which act through medicinal means such as pharmacological, metabolic or immunological methods.  The scope is inclusive of disposable devices as well as durable or persisting devices that require tracking, maintenance activities or regular calibration, recognising that each type of device has specific data recording requirements.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Current procedure']/items[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.procedure.v1]/description[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Hip joint implant']

localizedDescriptions.en: Identification of the medical device, preferably by a common name, a formal fully descriptive name or, if required, by class or category of device.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Current procedure']/items[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.procedure.v1]/description[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Hip joint implant']/items[at0001]/value

annotations.comment:
This data element will capture the term, phrase or category used in clinical practice. For example: <brand name><machine> (XYZ Audiometer); <size> <brand name> <intravenous catheter> (14G Jelco IV catheter); or <brand name/type> <implant>. With specific examples.

 Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible, although this may be local and depending on local supplies available.

localizedDescriptions.en: The number assigned by the manufacturer which identifies a group of items manufactured at the same time, usually found on the label or packaging material.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Current procedure']/items[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.procedure.v1]/description[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Hip joint implant']/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Inlay']/items[at0006]/value

children.name: Head component

localizedDescriptions.en: An instrument, apparatus, implant, material or similar, used in the provision of healthcare. In this context, a medical device includes a broad range of devices which act through a variety of physical, mechanical, thermal or similar means but specifically excludes devices which act through medicinal means such as pharmacological, metabolic or immunological methods.  The scope is inclusive of disposable devices as well as durable or persisting devices that require tracking, maintenance activities or regular calibration, recognising that each type of device has specific data recording requirements.

children.name: Device name

localizedDescriptions.en: Identification of the medical device, preferably by a common name, a formal fully descriptive name or, if required, by class or category of device.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Current procedure']/items[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.procedure.v1]/description[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Hip joint implant']/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Head component']/items[at0001]/value

annotations.comment:
This data element will capture the term, phrase or category used in clinical practice. For example: <brand name><machine> (XYZ Audiometer); <size> <brand name> <intravenous catheter> (14G Jelco IV catheter); or <brand name/type> <implant>. With specific examples.

 Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible, although this may be local and depending on local supplies available.

children.name: Type

localizedDescriptions.en: The category or kind of device.

annotations.comment:
Not applicable if a category is already recorded in 'Device name'.
 Example: if the 'Device' is named as a 'urinary catheter'; the 'Type' may be recorded as 'indwelling' or 'condom'.Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible. This may include use of GTIN or EAN numbers.
